identity.name: Skill
identity.definition: A Skill is a discrete learning competency, standard, or objective — such as a Common Core standard, state standard, or Renaissance learning progression — that represents what a student should know or be able to do, serving as the connective thread across assessments, assignments, resources, and scores in the Renaissance platform.

representations.heading: Card layout
representations.description: How a Skill appears in skill browsers, assignment builders, and standards dashboards

examples.name: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem
examples.subject: ELA

attributes.name: Skill Code
attributes.description: Unique identifier within the framework (e.g. 3.OA.A.1, RL.5.2). Primary visual identifier on cards.

attributes.name: Skill Name/Description
attributes.description: Human-readable description of what the skill covers.

attributes.name: Subject
attributes.description: Academic subject area: Math, ELA, Science, Social Studies.

attributes.name: Grade Level
attributes.description: Grade band(s) this skill applies to. A skill may span multiple grades.

attributes.name: Domain/Strand
attributes.description: Organizational grouping within the subject (e.g. Operations & Algebraic Thinking).

representations.heading: List layout
representations.description: How a Skill appears in standards tables, mastery reports, and alignment views

examples.name: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem
examples.subject: ELA

examples.name: Interpret products of whole numbers, e.g., interpret 5 × 7 as the total number of objects in 5 groups of 7 objects each.
examples.subject: Math

attributes.name: Skill Code
attributes.description: Unique identifier within the framework (e.g. 3.OA.A.1).

attributes.name: Skill Name/Description
attributes.description: Full description of the learning competency.

attributes.name: Subject
attributes.description: Academic subject area: Math, ELA, Science, Social Studies.

attributes.name: Grade Level
attributes.description: Grade band(s) this skill applies to.

attributes.name: Domain/Strand
attributes.description: Organizational grouping within the subject.

attributes.name: Standard Framework
attributes.description: CCSS, state-specific, or Renaissance learning progression.

attributes.name: Difficulty
attributes.description: Difficulty rating derived from assessment performance data. Scale 1-5.

attributes.name: Prerequisites
attributes.description: Skills that should be mastered before this one.

attributes.name: Related Skills
attributes.description: Skills that share conceptual overlap or cross-domain connections.

attributes.name: Status
attributes.description: Active or Deprecated. Deprecated skills remain for historical reporting but are hidden from new assignments.

stories.title: View Skill Mastery
stories.benefit: I can see which students have mastered, are progressing on, or are struggling with a specific standard so I can target my instruction.
stories.whenClause: I open a Skill from the standards browser,
stories.thenClause: the mastery distribution across my class, the number of related assessments and resources, and the skill's prerequisite chain.

stories.title: Assign Skill Practice
stories.benefit: students who haven't mastered the skill receive targeted practice through Freckle or Lalilo.
stories.whenClause: I select 'Assign Practice' on a Skill,
stories.thenClause: the system creates an adaptive assignment for selected students targeting that specific standard.

stories.title: Browse Standards Alignment
stories.benefit: I can verify that our assessment and curriculum content aligns with state and district standards.
stories.whenClause: I filter skills by framework and grade level,
stories.thenClause: all matching skills with their assessment coverage, resource counts, and mastery data across the district.

businessRules.title: Uniqueness
businessRules.description: A Skill is uniquely identified by its Skill Code within a Standard Framework. The combination of framework + code is globally unique across the system.

businessRules.title: Immutability
businessRules.description: Skill definitions are imported from external standards bodies (CCSS, state DOEs) and cannot be edited by users. Only Status can be changed (Active → Deprecated).

businessRules.title: Framework Alignment
businessRules.description: Each Skill belongs to exactly one Standard Framework. Cross-framework mappings (e.g. CCSS to a state standard) are maintained as Related Skills references.

businessRules.title: Mastery Calculation
businessRules.description: A student's mastery percentage for a Skill is system-calculated from assessment and practice Scores. The algorithm varies by product (Star uses IRT; Freckle uses adaptive practice streaks).

businessRules.title: Prerequisite Ordering
businessRules.description: Prerequisites form a directed acyclic graph. Circular dependencies are rejected at import. Prerequisite chains inform adaptive learning paths in Freckle and Lalilo.

businessRules.title: Deprecation
businessRules.description: When a standards framework is updated, superseded Skills are marked Deprecated. Historical Scores and mastery data persist, but the Skill no longer appears in assignment builders or active reports.

businessRules.title: Grade Spanning
businessRules.description: A Skill may apply to multiple grade levels (e.g. a progression standard spanning grades 3-5). Grade Level is stored as an array and filtering matches any grade in the range.

states.name: Active
states.description: Currently in use for assessments, assignments, and reporting

states.name: Deprecated
states.description: Superseded by updated standards; retained for historical data

relationships.mechanics: A Student's mastery of a Skill is system-calculated from assessment and practice Scores. Teachers view per-student mastery; students see their own proficiency in Freckle/Lalilo dashboards.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many. A Skill is measured across many Students; a Student works on many Skills. Typical: 50-200 skills per student per subject per year.
relationships.sorts: Students for a Skill: by mastery percentage (default), alphabetical, by grade level.
relationships.filters: By mastery band (Not started, Developing, Proficient, Advanced), by class, by grade level.
relationships.dependencies: If a Student is deactivated, their mastery data persists for historical reporting but is excluded from active class aggregations.

relationships.mechanics: Assessments measure one or more Skills. Star assessments map items to Skills via IRT; DnA assessments have explicit skill alignment set by the creator.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many. An Assessment measures multiple Skills; a Skill is measured by multiple Assessments.
relationships.sorts: Assessments for a Skill: by date administered (default), by assessment type, by average score.
relationships.filters: By assessment type (Star, DnA, Freckle), by date range, by class/school.
relationships.dependencies: If an Assessment is archived, its Scores still contribute to Skill mastery calculations.

relationships.mechanics: Resources are tagged with Skills they support. Teachers use Find Resources to discover content aligned to a specific Skill for remediation or enrichment.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many. A Resource supports multiple Skills; a Skill has multiple aligned Resources.
relationships.sorts: Resources for a Skill: by relevance (default), by type (video, article, activity), by grade level.
relationships.filters: By resource type, by product (myON, Freckle), by grade level, by language.
relationships.dependencies: If a Skill is deprecated, its resource alignments remain but the Skill is no longer surfaced in resource search results.

nestedObjects.name: Resource
nestedObjects.description: Content aligned to this skill for instruction or practice

nestedObjects.name: Score
nestedObjects.description: Assessment and practice scores that measure this skill

nestedObjects.name: Prerequisite Skill
nestedObjects.description: Skills that must be mastered before this one

allAttributes.name: Skill Code
allAttributes.description: Unique identifier within the framework (e.g. 3.OA.A.1, RL.5.2). Primary visual identifier across all representations.

allAttributes.name: Skill Name/Description
allAttributes.description: Human-readable description of the learning competency the skill represents.

allAttributes.name: Subject
allAttributes.description: Academic subject area: Math, ELA, Science, Social Studies. Determines color coding and report grouping.

allAttributes.name: Grade Level
allAttributes.description: Grade band(s) this skill applies to. A skill may span multiple grades (e.g. 3-5). PreK-12.

allAttributes.name: Domain/Strand
allAttributes.description: Organizational grouping within the subject (e.g. Operations & Algebraic Thinking, Reading: Literature).

allAttributes.name: Standard Framework
allAttributes.description: Which standards framework this skill belongs to: CCSS, state-specific, or Renaissance learning progression.

allAttributes.name: Difficulty
allAttributes.description: Difficulty rating derived from assessment performance data. Scale 1-5. Used by adaptive engines to sequence practice.

allAttributes.name: Prerequisites
allAttributes.description: Skills that should be mastered before this one. Forms a directed acyclic graph for learning path construction.

allAttributes.name: Related Skills
allAttributes.description: Skills that share conceptual overlap, cross-domain connections, or cross-framework equivalence.

allAttributes.name: Status
allAttributes.description: Active or Deprecated. Deprecated skills are retained for historical reporting but hidden from assignment builders and active reports.

structure.evidence: A Skill has a rich set of attributes: code, name/description, subject, grade level, domain/strand, framework, difficulty, prerequisites, related skills, and status. You can design a detail page for a Skill.

instances.evidence: 3.OA.A.1 (Interpret products of whole numbers), RL.5.2 (Determine a theme), 8.EE.B.5 (Graph proportional relationships). The Common Core alone contains thousands of Skill instances; state frameworks add thousands more.

purpose.evidence: Skills are the connective tissue of the Renaissance platform. Teachers view Skill mastery reports, assign practice by Skill, find resources aligned to Skills, and create assessments targeting specific Skills. Students see their Skill proficiency in dashboards.

sipValidation.verdict: Core knowledge object. The universal standard unit that connects assessments, assignments, resources, and scores.

synonyms.notes: The most common external synonym. Used in 'state standards', 'Common Core standards'.
Used when describing what students should be able to do after instruction.
Emphasizes mastery demonstration rather than coverage.
Technically a grouping level above individual skills, but often used interchangeably in casual conversation.
Similar to Domain — a mid-level organizational grouping. Usage varies by state.
Sometimes used to mean a Skill-level performance target rather than the Skill itself.

objectViews.description: Skills organized by subject and grade for browsing the standards framework. Teachers explore what skills exist and find aligned resources for instruction.
objectViews.userIntent: What skills exist in this subject and grade?

objectViews.description: Skills listed as data rows with class mastery percentages and student distribution. Teachers identify which skills the class has mastered and where to focus instruction next.
objectViews.userIntent: How has my class mastered each skill?

objectViews.description: The complete skill record — standards alignment, prerequisites, related skills, and difficulty. Teachers dig into a specific skill to plan instruction or assign targeted practice.
objectViews.userIntent: What is the full detail of this skill?

objectViews.description: Skills shown as compact cards with individual student mastery levels. Teachers check which skills a student has mastered and assign targeted practice where needed.
objectViews.userIntent: How has this student mastered each skill?

objectViews.description: A compact selection list of skills for targeting with an assignment. Shows mastery data and resource counts to help teachers choose the most impactful skills to practice.
objectViews.userIntent: Which skills should I target with this assignment?

objectViews.description: Skills measured by an assessment, shown with class-level performance data. Teachers see how students performed on each skill to identify instructional gaps.
objectViews.userIntent: How did students perform on each skill within this assessment?

objectViews.description: A skill shown as a section header within a student's proficiency view, identifying which skill is being profiled in the detail below.
Skills within a student's profile showing individual mastery percentages and proficiency bands. Teachers see at a glance which skills are solid and which need work.
objectViews.userIntent: How has this student mastered each skill?

studentMasteryPercent.description: Student mastery percentage for this skill

lastAssessedDate.description: Date of most recent assessment for this skill
